key_insight: The paper shows that nonconvex stochastic optimization can be reduced in a black-box way to ordinary static regret minimization, with the online learner choosing a preconditioner for the gradient tracker.
engineering_relevance: This gives a modular template for designing adaptive optimizers, helping practitioners plug in existing OCO methods to obtain provable convergence for smooth and nonsmooth nonconvex problems.
method: Maintain a predictable gradient tracker from stochastic gradients.
Use a black-box online convex learner to choose the preconditioner that maps the tracker to the update direction.
Prove convergence from only static-regret guarantees, including for Lipschitz nonconvex objectives and Goldstein stationarity.
evidence_signal: The abstract gives rate guarantees: for smooth objectives the method achieves an average squared-gradient bound with terms scaling like O(1/sqrt(T)) when the OCO oracle has O(sqrt(T)) regret, and for nonsmooth Lipschitz objectives it achieves the optimal O(T^(-2/7)) rate.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the online regret oracle and the stability of the preconditioner, so implementation details of the OCO subroutine may affect practical behavior.
paper_title: Optimizing the Preconditioner: A Black-box Online-to-Nonconvex Conversion with Static Regret Minimization Oracles
